title: BAD-AGENT DEMO: agent yields to a bare backchannel (FAIL: should hold, yielded)

transcript.agent: Great, so the next step is just to confirm your email and then...

why_it_matters: DELIBERATE BAD-AGENT RENDER, and the bad twin of bc-03. The caller only says 'mhm' but the agent stops mid-sentence and hands over the floor: did_yield is true where it must be false, so this should-hold event FAILS. This is the false-barge-in-on-a-backchannel axis. The scenario id contains no 'echo', so it is a genuine discrimination failure, not an audio-routing artifact, which is exactly the both-axes signal the fix map treats as an engagement-control problem.
